prevalence.source: ILAE-informed epilepsy epidemiology syntheses + regional context
prevalence.trend: stable; increased diagnosis with better imaging access

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to MRI/EEG
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ILAE criteria for epilepsy; focal seizure semiology; EEG and MRI for classification and etiology where available

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; seizure control depends on ASM access and specialist follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EEG/MRI access variable in SUS; specialist evaluation concentrated in capitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ASMs such as carbamazepine/levetiracetam; surgical evaluation for drug-resistant focal epilepsy in reference centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Treatment gap and limited access to epilepsy surgery programs outside large cities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialized centers exist and core ASMs are available
